statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 20 of 27 countries holding critical-level reserves in at least one fuel type, as stocks remain depleted following recent strategic reserve releases.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security remains under significant pressure in early June 2026, with the majority of EU member states operating below mandatory reserve requirements following recent strategic stock releases. The EU-wide averages of 96.3 days for petrol, 88.8 days for diesel, and 87.3 days for jet fuel mask severe disparities, with diesel and jet fuel both falling short of the required 90-day minimum.

The situation is most acute in Eastern Europe, where Poland, Romania, and Luxembourg are operating with critical reserves across all fuel categories. Poland's jet fuel reserves at just 22.5 days and Romania's diesel stocks at 30.2 days represent particularly concerning shortfalls. Meanwhile, larger economies including France, Germany, and Italy are also showing critical levels in multiple fuel types, indicating the breadth of supply strain across the continent.

The economic dimension adds further pressure, with Brent crude climbing 33.5% since the late-February escalation to $96.73 per barrel. This price surge is reflected in retail fuel costs, with petrol averaging €1.812 per liter EU-wide and significant variation between countries. Denmark faces the highest petrol prices at €2.392 per liter, while Malta benefits from the lowest at €1.34 per liter.

While some countries maintain comfortable reserves - particularly the Baltics and Nordic states - the overall picture shows a fuel security system under strain. The combination of depleted strategic reserves, uneven distribution of remaining stocks, and elevated global oil prices creates a challenging environment that requires continued monitoring and potential coordinated response measures.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries are operating with critical fuel reserves in at least one category, well below the 90-day minimum requirement
Diesel supplies are particularly strained with 18 countries below the mandatory 90-day threshold, averaging just 88.8 days across the EU
Jet fuel shows the most severe shortages, with 19 countries in critical status and EU average at 87.3 days
Oil prices have surged 33.5% since late February to $96.73/barrel, adding economic pressure alongside supply constraints
Eastern European countries face the most acute shortages, with Poland, Romania, and Luxembourg showing critical levels across all three fuel types
